content.thinking: Let me look at the chart image to answer the question about mill values and chained filter latency at P99.

The image shows charts with different mill values (4, 8, 12, 16 mill.) and latency measurements at P70 and P99 for both querying existing items and non-existing items. I need to find which mill value has the highest chained filter latency at P99.

The ChainedFilter is shown in light green/teal color. Looking at the charts:

(a) Query Existing Items:
- |T|=4 mill.: P99 looks around 21?


- |T|=8 mill.: P99 looks around 28?
- |T|=12 mill.: P99 looks around 24-26?
- |T|=16 mill.: P99 looks around 35?

(b) Query Non-Existing Items shows a similar pattern. Let me zoom in to get a clearer view of these values.

Now I can see the top row (Query Existing Items) more clearly. The y-axis values for ChainedFilter (light green/olive) at P99 for each mill value:

- |T|=4 mill.: y-axis range 9-72, ChainedFilter P99 looks to be around 21
- |T|=8 mill.: y-axis range 12-128, ChainedFilter P99 looks to be around 28
- |T|=12 mill.: y-axis range 15-180, ChainedFilter P99 looks to be around 33-35


- |T|=16 mill.: y-axis range 26-252, ChainedFilter P99 looks to be around 35

I should examine each chart more carefully to get precise readings. Let me look at the individual charts and measure the bar heights against their respective scales.

Now I can see the 16 mill. chart more clearly. The y-axis goes from 17 to 252 with markers at 17, 26, 35, and 252.

The ChainedFilter (light green color) at P99 looks like it reaches around 35-40. Let me look at the other mills too.

Actually wait - the legend says "ChainedFilter" is the light blue/grey color. But looking at the chart, I see different colors for different filters:
- Dark grey/black bottom: likely the base or No Filter
- Yellow/gold: Bloom Filter


- Light green: Bloom Filter (2x)
- Grey/light: ChainedFilter (the large area taking up the top)

Looking at the stacked bar chart for P99 at 16 mill., the ChainedFilter appears as that large dotted grey area reaching up to around 252 at the top. To answer which mill value has the highest chained filter latency at P99, I need to compare the absolute heights of the ChainedFilter sections across all the different mill values.

For the 16 mill. chart specifically, I'm seeing the y-axis marked at 17, 26, 35, and 252. The dashed grey area at the very top going up to 252 is likely the "No Filter" baseline, while the light green represents Bloom Filter, and the ChainedFilter (light blue/grey) sits around the 26-35 range. I'm realizing these might be stacked bars showing cumulative latency, or they could be overlaid areas showing total latency with each filter type layered on top. Let me look more carefully at how the bars are actually structured across the different mill values to determine which one has the highest ChainedFilter contribution. Looking at the non-existing items row (b), I see a similar pattern with latency increasing across the mill values. Comparing both query types, the 16 mill. configuration consistently shows the highest ChainedFilter P99 latency at around 35, so that's my answer.

Let me look at the 12 mill chart and then analyze all the values to determine which has the highest ChainedFilter P99 latency.

From what I can see:
- 4 mill.: y-axis 9-72, ChainedFilter P99 appears to be around 21 (the light grey/blue area)
- 8 mill.: y-axis 12-128, ChainedFilter P99 appears to be around 28


- 12 mill.: need to check
- 16 mill.: y-axis 17-252, ChainedFilter P99 appears to be around 35

Looking more carefully at these area charts, I realize each filter type is displayed as its own overlapping area rather than stacked sections, so I need to focus specifically on the light blue/grey ChainedFilter area across all four charts. The 16 mill. chart shows the highest ChainedFilter P99 value at approximately 35 μs.

Tracing through the pattern, the axis tick marks seem to correspond directly to the ChainedFilter P99 readings: 21 μs at 4 mill., 28 μs at 8 mill., and 35 μs at 16 mill., so I should double-check the 12 mill. chart to confirm where it falls in this progression.

Now I can see all 4 charts for row (a) - Query Existing Items:

- 4 mill.: y-axis shows 9, 15, 21, 72. ChainedFilter (light grey/dotted area) at P99 ≈ 21
- 8 mill.: y-axis shows 12, 20, 28, 128. ChainedFilter at P99 ≈ 28
- 12 mill.: y-axis shows 15, 24, 33, 180. ChainedFilter at P99 ≈ 33


- 16 mill.: y-axis shows 17, 26, 35, 252. ChainedFilter at P99 ≈ 35

The y-axis tick marks correspond to performance tiers: base latency, then Bloom Filter, then Bloom Filter (2x), then ChainedFilter, with NoFilter showing the highest values (72, 128, 180, 252) at the top.

Looking at how ChainedFilter scales across dataset sizes, the P99 latency climbs steadily from about 21 microseconds at 4 million entries up to 35 microseconds at 16 million, showing a consistent performance degradation as the dataset grows.
